text.title: Solo Burnout Tripwires — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every solo-founder weekly burnout-tripwire review must enforce. These are HARD rules — productive momentum cannot override them.

rule.statement: SLEEP TRIPWIRE: 7-day rolling mean of sleep duration MUST be ≥ 6h30m. Two consecutive weeks under 6h30m blocks new feature work until a recovery week is taken.

rationale.source: Walker, M. — Why We Sleep + multiple sleep-debt studies on cognitive performance

rule.rationale: Sub-6h30m mean produces decision-quality decline equivalent to legal intoxication; founders self-rate as "fine" up to the wall.

rule.statement: WEEKEND CREEP TRIPWIRE: ≤2 weekend days worked in a rolling 4-week window. The 3rd weekend-day fires a "schedule recovery" alert before the founder can plan further weekend work.

rationale.source: Pang, A. — Rest + bootstrap founder retrospectives (Indie Hackers + microconf surveys)

rule.rationale: Sustained weekend work correlates strongest with the "should I quit?" thought-spiral 6–8 weeks later; the rolling cap catches the slope early.

rule.statement: MRR-TO-EFFORT TRIPWIRE: A 3-month rolling change in (MRR / hours_worked) that is flat-or-declining triggers a "pricing or audience" review, not "work harder."

rationale.source: Founder-pricing literature (Cunningham et al., Patrick McKenzie writings)

rule.rationale: Burnout is often a price-of-effort problem, not an effort problem; the tripwire forces the right conversation.

rule.statement: JOY-CHANNEL TRIPWIRE: At least one non-product source of weekly joy (hobby, sport, social ritual) MUST be logged each week; two consecutive zero-weeks triggers a "schedule a non-product activity" item.

rationale.source: Self-determination theory / Ryan and Deci (2017) on autonomy + relatedness + competence

rule.rationale: Founders who lose all non-work identity collapse faster on minor product setbacks; the joy-channel buffer slows the collapse.

rule.statement: HEALTH-DEFERRAL TRIPWIRE: ≤2 deferred health appointments (doctor, dentist, eye, mental health) in the last 12 months; the 3rd deferral blocks new feature commits until one is booked.

rationale.source: Bootstrap-founder health-debt patterns (Tropical MBA / Bootstrapped.fm long-form interviews)

rule.rationale: Founders defer routine care because "next week is less busy"; the count is the only honest brake.

rule.statement: SOCIAL-CONTACT TRIPWIRE: ≥1 non-transactional human contact per week (in-person OR voice call, NOT a Slack DM); two consecutive zero-weeks fires "schedule a conversation" alert.

rationale.source: UCLA loneliness scale studies + remote-worker isolation research

rule.rationale: Solo isolation amplifies every other signal on this list 2–3x; the contact rule is cheap insurance.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.

example.title: Anchored weekly review
example.code: week_iso: 2026-W21 sleep_7d_mean_minutes: 412 # 6h52m, OK weekend_days_in_4w_window: 1 # OK mrr_to_hours_3m_slope: +0.04 # OK, rising joy_channel_logged: ["climbing-gym-tue", "dinner-w-mira-sat"] deferred_health_count_12m: 1 # OK non_transactional_contacts_w: 2 # OK verdict: green action: continue
example.note: All six tripwires green; the weekly review took less than two minutes.

example.title: Un-anchored review (post-hoc)
example.code: "Feeling tired but pushing through. Will rest after launch."
example.note: No measurement, no thresholds, deferred until "after." This is the burnout-debt curve in one sentence.
